title: Spectrophotometric Determination of V anadium(V) with N-Hydroxy-N-phenyl-N'-p-chlorophenyl-o-chlorobenzamidine Hydrochloride and Thiocyanate
author: L. P. CHANDRAKAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Ravishankar University, Raipur-492 010 

  Manuscript received 5 May 1983, revised 11 December 1983, accepted 9 March 1984  

 N-Hydroxy-N-phenyl-N ' - p -chlorophenyl- o -chlorobenzamidine hydrochloride (HPC-PCBH) is a newly synthesised reagent which reacts with vanadium(V) in presence of thiocyanate forming a green coloured water insoluble ternary complex quantitatively extractable into chloroform and shows  \(\lambda\)   max   in the region 600-620 om. The extractibility can be explained on the basis of the formation of 1 : 2 : 2 (metal : HPCPCBH : SCN - ) ternary species which is hydrophobic in nature. The method based on the sensitive colour reaction is fairly selective. The molar absorptivity and Sandell's sensitivity are 4713 I. mole -1  cm -1  and 0.018  \(\mu\) g/cm 2  of V(V), respectively. Beer's law is obeyed in the range 10 to 240  \(\mu\) g/25 ml. The applicability of the method is tested in BCS steel.
